description: Real-time bandwidth display (Task Group 7: full implementation). Bandwidth collector with tc output parsing, delta rate calculation, node push integration, panel-side storage, WebSocket broadcast, and tests.
steps: Create node/cmd/node/bandwidth.go (in package main, same as main.go) with: type UserBandwidth struct { IP string `json:"ip"`; ClassID string `json:"class_id"`; RxBytes int64 `json:"rx_bytes"`; TxBytes int64 `json:"tx_bytes"`; RxBps int64 `json:"rx_bps"`; TxBps int64 `json:"tx_bps"` }. type BandwidthCollector struct { prev map[string]UserBandwidth; lastCollect time.Time }. func NewBandwidthCollector() *BandwidthCollector. func (bc *BandwidthCollector) Collect(dev string) []UserBandwidth - runs 'tc -s class show dev <dev>' and parses output into per-class stats, then calls DeltaRates.

Implement parseTcOutput(output string) []tcClassStat that parses tc -s class show dev output. Each class entry looks like: 'class htb 1:NNN root/parent ... rate Xbit ...' followed by 'Sent NNNN bytes ...' lines. Parse class ID and byte counters.

Implement DeltaRates(current []tcClassStat, prev map[string]UserBandwidth, dt float64) []UserBandwidth that calculates per-second rates from byte counter deltas. Apply counter-wrap clamping: if delta is negative, set rate to 0.

In node/cmd/node/main.go Push struct, add: PerUserBandwidth []UserBandwidth `json:"per_user_bandwidth,omitempty"`. In the main loop, instantiate BandwidthCollector before the loop, call collector.Collect('tun0') on each cycle, and assign result to push.PerUserBandwidth.

Create migration panel/migrations/023_bandwidth_data.sql (next available number after 022): This is a no-op comment migration since 016_bandwidth_snapshots.sql already created the user_bandwidth_snapshots table. Just add a comment: '-- Bandwidth data extensions (base table already in 016_bandwidth_snapshots.sql)' and SELECT 1;

In panel/internal/api/api.go nodePush handler, add PerUserBandwidth field to the input struct. After the main transaction commit, if len(in.PerUserBandwidth) > 0, batch-insert into user_bandwidth_snapshots table (node_id, username mapped from IP via radacct framedipaddress lookup, ip, rx_bps, tx_bps).

In the realtimeWS handler (panel/internal/api/api.go around line 2479), in the ticker select case after sending 'sessions', add a new writeRealtime('bandwidth', s.bandwidthPayload()) call. Implement bandwidthPayload() that queries the latest user_bandwidth_snapshots (last 30 seconds, grouped by username) and returns []map[string]any with username, ip, rx_bps, tx_bps.
Write unit tests in node/cmd/node/bandwidth_test.go: test parseTcOutput with sample tc output, test DeltaRates with normal deltas and counter-wrap (negative delta clamped to 0).
acceptance_criteria: go test ./... passes including bandwidth parsing tests
DeltaRates handles counter wraps by clamping to 0
findings: All tests pass. Build succeeds. Implementation includes bandwidth.go with BandwidthCollector, parseTcOutput, DeltaRates; modified main.go Push struct and loop; modified api.go nodePush handler and realtimeWS handler; added bandwidthPayload method; created migration 023. Node tests verify parsing and counter-wrap clamping.
